topic: DeFi hacks & exploit losses over time (2020-2026)
summary: Crypto/DeFi theft losses grew explosively from ~$0.5B in 2020 to a then-record ~$3.8B in 2022 (Chainalysis, all crypto sectors). Losses then fell to ~$1.7B in 2023 before rebounding to ~$2.2B in 2024 and reaching a new record of ~$3.4B in 2025 (Chainalysis) / $3.35B (CertiK). The 2025 record was driven almost entirely by the single ~$1.5B Bybit exchange hack in February 2025 (the largest crypto theft ever), attributed to North Korea's Lazarus Group; excluding Bybit, 2025 would have shown a year-over-year decline. Sources differ on scope: Chainalysis and CertiK cover all crypto theft (CEX, bridges, DeFi, wallets); Immunefi tracks DeFi-protocol-only exploit losses, which peaked at $2.62B in 2022 and fell to $534M (2024) and $680M (2025), reflecting genuinely improved on-chain security despite rising TVL. DeFi-protocol losses specifically have become a shrinking share of total crypto theft, with attackers increasingly shifting to centralized services and individual wallets. Biggest single incidents: Bybit (~$1.5B, Feb 2025), Ronin Bridge (~$625M, Mar 2022), Poly Network (~$611M, Aug 2021, mostly returned), BNB Bridge (~$570M, Oct 2022), Wormhole (~$325M, Feb 2022), Euler (~$197M, Mar 2023, returned).

points.note: ~$520M total crypto stolen (of which ~$162M / 31% from DeFi); medium confidence on rounded total
